changes.finding: CONFIRMED HIGH. Production Knowledge Bus medical-intelligence assets are being opted into package manifests. Prompt §Classification note provides explicit rationale. SOP §10 HIGH triggers: production knowledge package content modification. Correct.

CONFIRMED CONTENT. ARCH-RT-5E audit (read in full) classifies PSI as 'deferred_non_launch_blocker'. PSI is not consumed by any launch-critical Intelligence Core module. Adding promoted_signal_intelligence: to a production manifest only triggers validation/status reporting. No emitted reasoning, scoring, DTOs, reports, frontend output, or user-visible behaviour changes. CONTENT classification is valid per SOP §4 definition and explicitly conditioned in prompt §Classification note.

changes.finding: CONFIRMED. Single authoritative source for PSI schema: knowledge_bus/schema/promoted_signal_intelligence_schema_v1.yaml. Single validator for PSI: backend/scripts/validate_promoted_signal_intelligence.py. Single production package store: knowledge_bus/packages/. Single orchestration validator: backend/scripts/validate_knowledge_package.py. No competing authority sources exist.

CONFIRMED from repository evidence. (1) load_promoted_signal_intelligence_for_package() is defined in backend/core/knowledge/load_promoted_signal_intelligence.py but is NOT imported by any launch-critical Intelligence Core module. (2) test_arch_rt5e_psi_runtime_wiring_decision.py enforces this as a machine-checked guard at CI (lines 28-34 assert no PSI import marker in 9 launch-critical paths). (3) ARCH-RT-5E audit table (lines 40-45) confirms: signal firing=No, card evidence=No, root-cause/WHY=No, report assembly=No, orchestrator=No. (4) The ARCH-RT-5E audit conclusion (line 50): 'PSI remains validation/compile-time artefact only.' (5) validate_knowledge_package.py PSI handling (lines 217-235): promoted_signal_intelligence key absent → status=SKIP; key present → run validate_promoted_signal_intelligence.py → PASS or FAIL. Effect is validation-only. Production PSI opt-in does NOT affect: signal activation, InsightGraph, root-cause output, report/compiler output, scoring, DTOs, frontend output, Gemini wording, or any backend/core runtime path.

changes.finding: CONFIRMED — exactly 20 production packages currently have promoted_signal_intelligence: in their manifests. All are pkg_kb47_* series. All 20 have matching promoted_signal_intelligence.yaml files on disk. Zero overlap with any of the 22 activation-ready candidates (which are all pkg_kb52c_*, pkg_kb60_*, pkg_kb61_* series). Existing opt-ins predate this sprint and are not affected by P1-15.

changes.finding: CONFIRMED — all 22 production package directories from the P1-14 activation-ready cohort exist in knowledge_bus/packages/. Sample spot-check: pkg_kb52c_alt_high_hepatocellular_injury_pattern/package_manifest.yaml (read at line 1): package_id confirmed, behavioural_impact: NONE, no promoted_signal_intelligence key present. Grep of knowledge_bus/packages for promoted_signal_intelligence in pkg_kb52c_*, pkg_kb60_*, pkg_kb61_* returns zero matches — confirming no existing PSI opt-in for any of the 22 target packages.

changes.finding: CONFIRMED. Existing pkg_kb47_egfr_low_hemodynamic_filtration_drop/package_manifest.yaml (read at line 7) shows: 'promoted_signal_intelligence: promoted_signal_intelligence.yaml' — relative path format. The behavioural_impact: NONE field is retained unchanged alongside the PSI key. Adding PSI does NOT require changing behavioural_impact. The PSI key value must be the relative path to the PSI file within the package directory.

CONFIRMED. Staged PSI paths: knowledge_bus/generated_pilot/<batch>/<package_id>/promoted_signal_intelligence.yaml. Production package paths: knowledge_bus/packages/<package_id>/. Package IDs match exactly (e.g., pkg_kb52c_alt_high_hepatocellular_injury_pattern appears in both staged and production). Copy operation: read staged PSI → place in production package directory as promoted_signal_intelligence.yaml → add manifest key. Staged file must remain unmodified.

CONFIRMED. The only changes within production packages are: (1) new file: promoted_signal_intelligence.yaml (PSI content copy, no modification); (2) addition of promoted_signal_intelligence: field in package_manifest.yaml. Both are CONTENT per SOP §4 definition (non-executable data, governed content, YAML modification). The CONTENT classification is valid only if STOP gate 1 confirms no runtime effect — which it does (see stop_gate_1_runtime_boundary above).

Adding PSI to 22 packages will change classify_package_type() return value from 'signal-only' to 'WHY-enabled' for all 22 (kb_lifecycle_contract_v1.py:171-174). iter_why_enabled_packages() (called at test_lc_s16_17_19_kb_surface_payload_contract.py:247) will iterate 22 additional packages. That regression test validates WHY-enabled package contract structure. If any of the 22 staged PSI files fail the KB surface payload contract assertions, STOP gate 5 will fire. Cursor should run 'pytest backend/tests/regression/test_lc_s16_17_19_kb_surface_payload_contract.py' as part of Phase 5 in addition to the three listed validators. NOT a blocker — STOP gate 5 covers this — but Phase 5 is safer with regression tests included.

infer_governed_tier() in kb_lifecycle_contract_v1.py (lines 223-231) grants 'v3_grade_rich_governed' tier only to pkg_kb47_* packages with PSI. For pkg_kb52c_*, pkg_kb60_*, pkg_kb61_* packages, the function returns 'post_kb_s49_unreviewed_batch' regardless of PSI status. Adding PSI to the 22 candidates will NOT promote them to 'v3_grade_rich_governed'. They will show package_type: WHY-enabled but governed_tier: post_kb_s49_unreviewed_batch in estate inventory tooling. Cursor should confirm test_lc_s18a_package_estate_inventory.py still passes after opt-in.

changes.result: CONFIRMED — PROMPT STOP GATES ENFORCE THIS
changes.finding: CONFIRMED. The 19 blocked candidates are named explicitly in P1-14 cohort manifest with machine-readable blocker fields. STOP gate 3 fires if zero READY_FOR_OPT_IN candidates exist. STOP gate 4 fires if any opted-in PSI would require content modification to pass validation. Sprint acceptance criteria items 4 and 5 reiterate: only activation-ready candidates may be opted in; identity-blocked, derived-marker-blocked and medical-review-blocked candidates must not be promoted. Prompt structure is sound for this protection.

changes.finding: CONFIRMED. Prompt §Forbidden files (lines 283-301) explicitly prohibits modification of: backend/core/, backend/scripts/, backend/tests/, backend/ssot/, frontend/, knowledge_bus/generated_pilot/**/promoted_signal_intelligence.yaml (staged PSI must not be edited), knowledge_bus/generated_pilot/**/compile_manifest.yaml, knowledge_bus/research/, docs/strategy/beta_readiness/HEALTHIQ_AI_BETA_READINESS_DEFINITIVE_STRATEGY_FINAL_2026-06-20.md, automation_bus/latest_gate_evidence.json, automation_bus/latest_gate_output.txt. Tooling files (.codex/, .cursor/, .vscode/, AGENTS.md) must not enter the sprint branch. Permitted production package writes are scoped to: new promoted_signal_intelligence.yaml in target package dirs; promoted_signal_intelligence: manifest key in package_manifest.yaml for target packages; new documentation files.

All validation commands in the prompt use standard Python invocation and git commands compatible with PowerShell. No bash-specific syntax used. Commands such as 'python backend/scripts/validate_staged_psi_activation_readiness.py', 'python backend/scripts/validate_knowledge_package.py --package-dir <path>', and 'python backend/scripts/validate_promoted_signal_intelligence.py --model <path>' are all PowerShell-compatible.

overall_verdict: HARDENED. All 5 front-matter fields confirmed present and valid. All 8 mandatory authority files confirmed to exist in repository. STOP gate 1 evidence conclusive: ARCH-RT-5E audit + machine-enforced test guard confirm PSI is validation/compile-time only — no runtime, scoring, DTO, report, frontend, or Gemini effect. change_type: CONTENT validated as correct against this evidence. risk_level: HIGH confirmed appropriate for production KB medical asset modification. Exactly 20 existing PSI opt-ins (all pkg_kb47_*) confirmed with zero overlap against the 22 activation-ready candidates. All 22 target production package directories confirmed to exist with no existing PSI opt-in. Manifest opt-in format confirmed from pkg_kb47_* precedent. Two soft flags documented (regression test scope expansion, tier classification note) — both covered by STOP gate 5; neither is a blocker. Cursor may proceed under TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH governance.
